NOVA ENERGIJA. {{!}} NOVI DRES SRBIJE, DIZAJN KOJI SPAJA TRADICIJU I INOVACIJU {{!}} Fudbalski savez Srbije |url=https://fss.rs/nova-era-nova-boja-nova-energija-novi-dres-srbije-dizajn-koji-spaja-tradiciju-i-inovaciju/?script=lat |access-date=2025-09-09 |language=sr-RS}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Nedeljković |first=Vojislav |date=2025-08-12 |title=Fudbaleri Srbije imaju novi dres - po prvi put će igrati u ovoj boji FOTO |url=https://nova.rs/sport/fudbal/srbija-plavi-dres-svetsko-prvenstvo/ |access-date=2025-09-09 |website=NOVA portal |language=sr-RS}}</ref> This decision was mostly approved by the public.<ref>{{Cite web |last=d.o.o |first=cubes |title=Reprezentacija u fudbalu ponovo u plavim dresovima - Nedeljnik |url=https://www.nedeljnik.rs/reprezentacija-u-fudbalu-ponovo-u-plavim-dresovima/ |access-date=2025-09-09 |website=www.nedeljnik.rs/ |language=sr-RS}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=d.o.o |first=cubes |title=Kako je Srbija poplavela - Velike Priče |url=https://velikeprice.com/sport/srbija-novi-dresovi-fudbal-plavi/ |access-date=2025-09-09 |website=velikeprice.com/ |language=sr-RS}}</ref>

[[File:Serbia - Slovenija football match in Belgrade, 2010.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The 53,000-capacity [[Red Star Stadium|Rajko Mitić Stadium]] is the largest in Serbia, and is often utilised for international fixtures.]]

Serbia does not have an official national stadium and the team has played at grounds throughout the country. The [[Red Star Stadium|Rajko Mitić Stadium]] is the most popular venue following by [[Partizan Stadium]], both ground are located in the capital city [[Belgrade]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Serbia National Stadium - Belgrade - The Stadium Guide|url=https://www.stadiumguide.com/serbia-national-stadium/|access-date=21 October 2021|language=nl|archive-date=12 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211012193920/https://www.stadiumguide.com/serbia-national-stadium/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=New design: "Serbian Wembley", Vučić's grand vision – StadiumDB.com|url=http://stadiumdb.com/news/2021/01/new_design_serbian_wembley_vucics_grand_vision|access-date=21 October 2021|website=stadiumdb.com|archive-date=21 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021120624/http://stadiumdb.com/news/2021/01/new_design_serbian_wembley_vucics_grand_vision|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=J.M.|date=20 March 2019|title=Marakana kroz istoriju - najveći srpski stadion |url=https://convivo.rs/sports/marakana/|access-date=21 October 2021|website=Convivo|language=sr-cyrl|archive-date=21 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021120624/https://convivo.rs/sports/marakana/|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Kit sponsorship===
{{Commons|Serbia national football team kits}}
In July 2014, a partnership was announced between the [[Football Association of Serbia]] and English manufacturer [[Umbro]], which is Serbia's official supplier, before [[Puma (brand)|Puma]] took over with their home and away kits, debuting 7 September 2014 in the friendly match against [[France national football team|France]]. That same day, Serbia unveiled their latest kits also worn at the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group I|Euro 2016 qualifiers campaign]].<ref>[http://www.football-shirts.co.uk/fans/serbia-sign-new-kit-deal-with-umbro_25575 Serbia set to sign new kit deal with Umbro?] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140729204800/http://www.football-shirts.co.uk/fans/serbia-sign-new-kit-deal-with-umbro_25575 |date=29 July 2014 }} Football-shirts.co.uk 6 March 2014</ref> In January 2025, the Football Association of Serbia announced [[Capelli Sport]] as the new sponsor of the national team, replacing Puma.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.footyheadlines.com/2024/12/serbia-macron-kit-deal.html|title=No More Puma: Serbia Announces Capelli Kit Deal|work=FootyHeadlines|date=23 January 2025|accessdate=21 March 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.footyheadlines.com/2025/01/serbia-2025-home-away-kits.html|title=Exclusive: Capelli Serbia 2025 Home & Away Kits Leaked - No More Puma|work=FootyHeadlines|date=23 January 2025|accessdate=21 March 2025}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=d.o.o |first=cubes |title=Kako je Srbija poplavela - Velike Priče |url=https://velikeprice.com/sport/srbija-novi-dresovi-fudbal-plavi/ |access-date=2025-09-09 |website=velikeprice.com/ |language=sr-RS}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
! Kit Supplier
! Period
|-
|align="Left"| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]] ||1974–2002
|-
|align="Left"| {{flagicon|ITA|2003}} [[Lotto Sport Italia|Lotto]] ||2002–2006
|-
|align="Left"| {{flagicon|USA}} [[Nike, Inc.|Nike]] ||2006–2014
|-
|align="Left"| {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Umbro]] ||2014–2018
|-
|align="Left"| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Puma (company)|Puma]] ||2018–2025
|-
|align="Left"| {{flagicon|USA}} [[Capelli Sport]] ||2025–present
|}

== Results and fixtures ==
{{Main|Serbia national football team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date       = 4 June 2026
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-6}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MEX}}
|score      = 5−1
|team2      = {{fb|SRB}}

== Management ==
{{updated|12 October 2025|<ref>{{cite web|url=https://fss.rs/selekcije/a-selekcija/?script=lat|title=Stručni štab|website=fss.rs|language=sr|access-date=10 September 2025}}</ref>}}

|+ Coaching staff
! Position 
! Name
|-
| Head coach
|{{flagicon|SRB}} [[Veljko Paunović]]
|-
| Director of the A team
|{{flagicon|SRB}} [[Stevan Stojanović]]
|-
| Assistant coach(es)
| {{flagicon|POR}} [[Nuno Gomes (footballer, born 1978)|Nuno Gomes]]<hr />{{flagicon|RSA}} [[Quinton Fortune]]<hr />{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Claudio Arzeno]]<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} [[Aleksandar Rogić]]

|-
| Goalkeeping coach
| {{flagicon|SPA}} Jesús Salvador Garrido
|-
| Analyst
| {{flagicon|SRB}} Lazar Tomić<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} Bogdan Milićević
|-
| Team manager(s)
| {{flagicon|SRB}} Nemanja Filipović<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} Dušan Gelić
|-
| Doctor(s)
|{{flagicon|SRB}} Dejan Aleksandrić <br> {{flagicon|SRB}} Ilija Rosić
|-
| Physiotherapist(s)
| {{flagicon|SRB}} Goran Zuvić<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} Dejan Bogdanović<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} Viktor Vujošević<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} Zoran Vujić<hr />{{flagicon|SRB}} Nemanja Božić

| Economist(s)

| Security commissioner
| {{flagicon|SRB}} Ivan Petrić
|-
| Spokesperson
| {{flagicon|SRB}} Milan Vuković
|}

==Players==
===Current squad===
*The following players were called up for the Friendly matches against [[Cape Verde national football team|Cape Verde]] and [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]], on 31 May and 4 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://fss.rs/selektor-veljko-paunovic-sopstio-spisak-igraca-za-dve-prijateljske-utakmice-zelenortskih-ostrva-31-maja-i-meksika-5-juna|title=СЕЛЕКТОР ВЕЉКО ПАУНОВИЋ СAОПШТИО СПИСАК ИГРАЧА ЗА ДВЕ ПРИЈАТЕЉСКЕ УТАКМИЦЕ, ПРОТИВ ЗЕЛЕНОРТСКИХ ОСТРВА 31. МАЈА И МЕКСИКА 5. ЈУНА|language=sr-Lat|publisher=[[Football Association of Serbia]]|date=26 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://fss.rs/petrovic-umesto-milosavljevica|title=ПЕТРОВИЋ УМЕСТО МИЛОСАВЉЕВИЋА|language=sr-Lat|publisher=[[Football Association of Serbia]]|date=27 May 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://fss.rs/posle-utakmice-u-lisabonu-tri-igraca-oslobodjena-obaveza-za-meksiko|title=ПОСЛЕ УТАКМИЦЕ У ЛИСАБОНУ ТРИ ИГРАЧА ОСЛОБОЂЕНА ОБАВЕЗА ЗА МЕКСИКО|language=sr-Lat|publisher=[[Football Association of Serbia]]|date=31 May 2026}}</ref>
*''Caps and goals are correct {{as of|2026|6|4|lc=y}}, after the match against [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]].''

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for the team within the last twelve months and are still available for selection.

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Filip Kostić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1992|11|1|df=y}}|caps=72|goals=3|club=[[Free agent|''Unattached'']]|clubnat=|other=[[Captain (association football)|vice-captain]]|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Saša Lukić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|8|13|df=y}}|caps=62|goals=2|club=[[Fulham F.C.|Fulham]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nemanja Maksimović]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|1|26|df=y}}|caps=60|goals=1|club=[[Shabab Al-Ahli]]|clubnat=UAE|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Sergej Milinković-Savić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|2|27|df=y}}|caps=56|goals=9|club=[[Al Hilal SFC|Al-Hilal]]|clubnat=KSA|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mijat Gaćinović]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|2|8|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=2|club=[[AEK Athens F.C.|AEK Athens]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Lazar Samardžić]]|age={{Birth date and age|2002|2|24|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=1|club=[[Atalanta BC|Atalanta]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nemanja Radonjić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|2|15|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=5|club=[[Free agent|''Unattached'']]|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|LAT}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Marko Grujić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1996|4|13|df=y}}|caps=30|goals=0|club=[[AEK Athens F.C.|AEK Athens]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|LAT}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ivan Ilić (footballer, born 2001)|Ivan Ilić]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|3|17|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=[[Torino FC|Torino]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|LAT}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Aleksandar Katai]]|age={{Birth date and age|1991|2|6|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[Red Star Belgrade]]|clubnat=SRB|latest=v. {{fb|LAT}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Luka Ilić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|7|2|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[Real Oviedo|Oviedo]]|clubnat=ESP|latest=v. {{fb|LAT}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Stefan Mitrović (footballer, born August 2002)|Stefan Mitrović]]|age={{Birth date and age|2002|8|15|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Asteras Tripolis F.C.|Asteras Tripolis]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|AND}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ognjen Ugrešić]]|age={{Birth date and age|2006|7|15|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FK Partizan|Partizan]]|clubnat=SRB|latest=v. {{fb|AND}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Uroš Račić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1998|3|17|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Aris Thessaloniki F.C.|Aris]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|ENG}}, 9 September 2025}}

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jovan Milošević]]|age={{Birth date and age|2005|7|31|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[SV Werder Bremen|Werder Bremen]]|clubnat=GER|latest=v. {{fb|CPV}}, 31 May 2026<sup>WD</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Aleksandar Mitrović]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|9|16|df=y}}|caps=106|goals=64|club=[[Al-Rayyan SC|Al-Rayyan]]|clubnat=QAT|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Luka Jović]]|age={{Birth date and age|1997|12|23|df=y}}|caps=52|goals=11|club=[[AEK Athens F.C.|AEK Athens]]|clubnat=GRE|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dejan Joveljić]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|8|7|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=2|club=[[Sporting Kansas City]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Petar Ratkov]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|8|18|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[SS Lazio|Lazio]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|KSA}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dušan Vlahović]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|1|28|df=y}}|caps=41|goals=16|club=[[Free agent|''Unattached'']]|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|LAT}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mihajlo Cvetković]]|age={{Birth date and age|2007|1|10|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[RSC Anderlecht|Anderlecht]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|AND}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Andrej Ilić]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|4|3|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[1. FC Union Berlin|Union Berlin]]|clubnat=GER|latest=v. {{fb|AND}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes:
* <sup>INJ</sup> = Not part of the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to injury.
* <sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
<!-- * <sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team.
* <sup>SUS</sup> = Not available in next match due to red card suspension.
* <sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the current squad due to non-injury issue. -->
{{nat fs end}}

{{col-end}}

==Player records==
{{updated|31 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Yugoslavia (Serbia (and Montenegro)) - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/joeg-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=27 November 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221127154643/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/joeg-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Serbia.''

== Notable players ==
{{for|notable players of [[Yugoslavia national football team|Yugoslavia]]|Yugoslavia national football team#Notable_players}}

;Goalkeepers
<gallery>
File:Ivica Kralj, 2009.jpg|[[Ivica Kralj]] played for the team from 1996 to 2001
File:Vladimir Stojković 2018 (cropped).jpg|[[Vladimir Stojković]] is the most capped goalkeeper in the team's history with 84 caps
</gallery>

;Defenders
<gallery>
File:Miroslav Đukić.jpg|[[Miroslav Đukić]] played for the team from 1991 to 2001
File:Siniša Mihajlović (cropped).JPG|[[Siniša Mihajlović]] played 63 matches for the team from 1993 to 2003 and managed the team in [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2014 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
File:Zoran Mirkovic - Commons photo (cropped).jpg|[[Zoran Mirković]] played for the team from 1995 to 2003
File:Mladen Krstajic (cropped).jpg|[[Mladen Krstajić]] played 59 matches for the team from 1999 to 2008 and managed the team at the [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018 World Cup]]
File:Vidić (cropped).jpg|[[Nemanja Vidić]] played 56 matches, was a participant in two World Cups and named twice in the [[FIFPro#FIFA FIFPro World XI|FIFA World XI]]
File:Subotic170727.jpg|[[Neven Subotić]] played 36 matches, was a participant at the [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010 World Cup]]
File:Cos-Serb (2).jpg|[[Branislav Ivanović]] played 105 matches for the team from 2005 to 2018
File:Aleksandar Kolarov 2018.jpg|[[Aleksandar Kolarov]] played 94 matches and captained the team at the 2018 World Cup
</gallery>

;Midfielders
<gallery>
File:Dragan Stojkovic Piksi 2024.png|[[Dragan Stojković]] played from 1983 to 2001 for the team, and managed the team from 2021 to 2025
File:Dejan Savićević.jpg|[[Dejan Savićević]] played for the team from 1986 to 1999 and managed the team from 2001 to 2003
File:Jugovic 2019.jpg|[[Vladimir Jugović]] played for the team from 1991 to 2002
File:DK-Maccabi (10).jpg|[[Slaviša Jokanović]] played for the team from 1991 to 2002
File:Dejan_Stanković_-_Inter_Mailand_%284%29.jpg|[[Dejan Stanković]] is the Serbian player who won the most trophies; he played in three World Cups and one European Championship
File:MilosKrasic.jpg|[[Miloš Krasić]] played for the team from 2006 to 2011
File:Jovanovic (cropped).JPG|[[Milan Jovanović (footballer, born 1981)|Milan Jovanović]] played for the team from 2007 to 2012
File:Ser-Swi (27).jpg|[[Nemanja Matić]] played for the team from 2008 to 2019
File:Dušan Tadić (cropped).jpg|[[Dušan Tadić]] is the most capped player in the team's history with 111 caps from 2008 to 2024
File:2022128173937 2022-05-08 Fussball Eintracht Frankfurt vs Borussia Mönchengladbach - Sven - 1D X MK II - 2399 - B70I8510 (Filip Kostić cropped).jpg| [[Filip Kostić]] has been playing for the team since 2015
</gallery>

;Forwards
<gallery>
File:Predrag Mijatović 2007 b.jpg|[[Predrag Mijatović]] was a top goalscorer in 1998 World Cup qualifiers with 14 goals
File:Savo Milosevic (cropped).jpg|[[Savo Milošević]] played 102 matches, scored 37 goals and was [[UEFA Euro 2000#Goalscorers|UEFA Euro 2000 Golden Boot]]
File:Darko Kovačević 5-mar-2008.jpg|[[Darko Kovačević]] played 59 matches and scored 10 goals between 1994 and 2004
File:MatejaKezman.jpg|[[Mateja Kežman]] was the top goal scorer for the team in [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2006 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
File:Zigic vs Antwerp (cropped).jpg|[[Nikola Žigić]] played 57 matches and scored 20 goals between 2004 and 2011
File:Aleksandar Mitrović 2018 (cropped).jpg|[[Aleksandar Mitrović]] is top goal scorer in the history of national team, and was [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League|2018–19]] and [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League|2022–23]] UEFA [[UEFA Nations League|Nations League]] top goalscorer
</gallery>

==Competitive record==
Serbia was part of [[Yugoslavia]] and its [[Yugoslavia national football team|national football team]] which existed between 1920 and 1992. With the [[Breakup of Yugoslavia|collapse of Yugoslavia]], the remaining constituent republics, [[Republic of Serbia (1992–2006)|Serbia]] and [[Republic of Montenegro (1992–2006)|Montenegro]], formed the [[Serbia and Montenegro|Federal Republic of Yugoslavia]] in 1992. The country underwent a name change in 2003, and Montenegro left the state union in 2006. [[FIFA]] and [[UEFA]] consider the [[Football Association of Serbia]] a direct successor to both the [[Football Association of Yugoslavia]] and the [[Football Association of Serbia and Montenegro]], thereby attributing all records to Serbia.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2018-10-13 |title=Bilans protiv reprezentacija 1920-2019 |url=https://www.reprezentacija.rs/bilans-protiv-reprezentacija-1920-danas/ |access-date=2022-11-22 |website=reprezentacija.rs |language=en-US |archive-date=22 November 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221122224039/https://www.reprezentacija.rs/bilans-protiv-reprezentacija-1920-danas/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="width:50%;text-align:center"
|-
! colspan="2"|'''Serbia's Nations League record'''
|-
!First match
|{{fb|Lithuania}} 0–1 {{fb-rt|Serbia}}<br />([[Vilnius]], Lithuania; 7 September 2018)
|-
!Biggest Win
|{{fb|Serbia}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|Russia}}<br />([[Belgrade]], Serbia; 18 November 2020)
|-
!Biggest Defeat
|{{fb|Russia}} 3–1 {{fb-rt|Serbia}}<br /> ([[Moscow]], Russia; 3 September 2020)<br /> {{fb|Denmark}} 2–0 {{fb-rt|Serbia}}<br /> ([[Copenhagen]], Denmark; 8 September 2024)
|-
!Best Result
|10th in [[2024–25 UEFA Nations League|2024–25]]
|-
!Worst Result
|27th in [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League|2018–19]] and [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League|2020–21]]
|}

==Head-to-head records (2006 onwards)==
{{further|Serbia national football team results}}
<section begin=head-to-head/>{{#section:Serbia national football team results|head-to-head}}<section end=head-to-head/>

== Honours ==
===Global===
* '''[[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic Games]]'''{{efn-ua|name="allyugoslavia"|All honours won as Yugoslavia}}
** {{gold1}} '''Gold Medal (1):''' [[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics|1960]]
** {{silver2}} Silver Medal (3): [[Football at the 1948 Summer Olympics|1948]], [[Football at the 1952 Summer Olympics|1952]], [[Association football at the 1956 Summer Olympics|1956]]
** {{bronze3}} Bronze Medal (1): [[Football at the 1984 Summer Olympics|1984]]

===Continental===
* '''[[UEFA European Championship]]'''{{efn-ua|name="allyugoslavia"}}
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[1960 European Nations' Cup|1960]], [[UEFA Euro 1968|1968]]

===Regional===
* '''[[Balkan Cup]]'''{{efn-ua|name="allyugoslavia"}}
** '''Champions (2):''' [[1934–35 Balkan Cup|1934–35]], 1935
** Runners-up (6): [[1929–31 Balkan Cup|1929–31]], 1932, 1933, 1946, 1947, [[1977–80 Balkan Cup|1977–80]]
** Third place (1): [[1931 Balkan Cup|1931]]
* '''[[Football at the Mediterranean Games|Mediterranean Games]]'''{{efn-ua|name="allyugoslavia"}}
** '''Gold medal (2)''': [[1971 Mediterranean Games|1971]], [[1979 Mediterranean Games|1979]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[1945 Yugoslav Football Tournament]]'''{{efn-ua|name="allyugoslavia"}}
** '''Champions (1):''' 1945
* '''[[Lunar New Year Cup]]'''
** '''Champions (1):''' [[1995 Lunar New Year Cup|1995]]
* '''[[Millennium Super Soccer Cup|Millennium Super Cup]]'''
** '''Champions (1):''' 2001
* '''[[Korea Cup]]'''
** Runners-up (1): [[1997 Korea Cup|1997]]
* '''[[Kirin Cup Soccer|Kirin Cup]]'''
** Runners-up (1): 2004

===Awards===
* '''[[FIFA World Rankings#Best Mover of the Year|FIFA Best Mover of the Year]]''': 1997

;Unofficial
